How's it going everybody? In this video I'm going to give you a brief rundown of what you would need to change in one of these example funnels to make it a little more ready for your gym to use. Now always keep in mind these are examples. You should look into making your own funnels and I do also recommend creating a blank funnel and trying to make one from scratch. That way you're a little more familiar with the process and you aren't working with something that somebody else has already edited. So I've opened up the six-week challenge funnel, the 42-day challenge funnel. The process is going to be similar for other funnels that we have. This is just an example. Once you get that funnel opened up go and hit this edit page button, that yellow edit page. And now we're at the funnel editor. So inside of ClickFunnels they organize things into kind of group sections. So we have this section here, we have a section there, and then inside of that section. And you notice there are different colors. So there's the green, the blue, and the orange. And that kind of lets you know where things are divided into. You can add new sections underneath any of these by hitting the plus and it'll tell you what it is. So that adds a new element, the orange one, that adds a new row, the blue one, and that one adds a new section, the green one. So that's kind of the brief rundown of how they organize this. The bar at the top gives you some really cool features. This lets you switch between mobile and desktop view. I always recommend before you start sending ads to a funnel, make sure it looks good in mobile, make sure it works in mobile. That way you don't have to worry about headaches because a lot of ads go to mobile nowadays. I'm going to switch back to desktop just so we can see everything really well. I'm going to go through some of the things in the settings menu that you need to change. So inside of settings, look for this SEO metadata and when you click on that, it's going to open the sidebar on the right. And that's a very common theme inside of ClickFunnels. You click on something, you get a sidebar on the right that gives you more options. So now that we have this SEO metadata open, I'm going to change this title. This is what the actual page says up at the top. So let's go ahead and call this. And of course, you want to make that custom for your gem. Description is going to be what shows up if it shows up in like a Google search result or if you post the link somewhere that it gives a preview of the page. So it's a good idea to do a good description. Of course, use whatever fits best for you. Keywords and author aren't very important unless you're expecting this to be found in Google search results or Yahoo search results. But make sure your search results are relevant if you're going to use it. So let's go ahead and do... So just kind of look through some keywords that are relevant to your gem, things that you would expect people to search for to find you. Author can really be anything you want. A lot of times, you just want to use your gem name there. And social image is going to be the image that shows up when they see the page on these previews. So if say, for instance, you post the link on Facebook, a lot of times Facebook is going to show an image directly with that link. And this is going to be what shows up. I recommend using something like a gem logo or if you made an image just for this challenge, you can click that little icon next to it to the right. It's going to take you to the image uploader. The library shows all of your images. If you want to upload a new image, you can just click that upload, pick the image you want, and hit open. It's going to upload that image to your library. There's your placeholder image. It'll select automatically. It has that blue border around if it's selected. And then if you're ready to use that image, you can just click this green add image button. And if you look below, it shows the SEO preview, the search engine optimization preview. And you can see what it's going to look like. That's great. You've gotten that SEO metadata updated. We're good to go there. Go and click back. Now, the people in the photo in the background are probably not your gem members. So you want to change that to something that's relevant for your gem. To change the background image, you go to the settings menu. You're going to click on background right there. And here it says background image, there's an image already there. Click that button there to the right of it. And choose a different image. I'm going to go and choose that one add image. Now, if you notice this image doesn't look very good. So under image position, I'm going to change some of these options to find the one that looks the best. I think that one looks the best there. If you don't like it, you can always change it to a different image by clicking that. Now, background color is going to be important if you have this running over. So you want to pick something that's a closer image. This is a pretty dark image. So for background color, I'm going to go and choose the black right there. I'm going to click back. And there we go. I don't recommend doing a YouTube video for your background. It's going to make your page lower to load up. And it's something that can be very distracting to have that image moving behind the text. Once we're done with the background, I'm going to click back on the page and that bar disappears in our background set. So one of the things that we note for you is that you need to update your privacy policy in your terms of use before using that. So we give you some instructions how to do that. I'm also going to show you. So you need the link to your privacy policy or your terms of use. And that's going to be your website where you have it. You can also put it on my click funnel and get the link and share that link there. So I'm going to scroll down to the bottom. And there's the privacy policy button. I'm going to go and click on that. And it opens up the sidebar to edit this button settings. So next to edit action, I'm going to click this set action. It's set to go to a website URL, which is what we want. I'm going to click in this box. I'm going to delete the little hashtag placeholder. And you want to go and put your link to your privacy policy in here. Now, right here where it says open and same window, you want to change that into open and new tab slash window. That way, if they click on that link, it's not going to close them out of where they were. It's going to leave it open and they can go back to it. So I'm going to go and hit the back and click back here. And now instead of showing that warning, it shows the website that it goes to. So I'm going to do the same for the terms of use. Clicking on the button, I'm going to click the green set action, making sure it's on go to website URL. I'm going to delete what's in that box and go and type in the URL of my terms of use. And then change that from open and same window to open and new window. That looks good. So I'm going to hit back back again. You can see there it now shows that link. So now that we've gotten that taken care of, we can delete this text. So I'm going to move my mouse over this section. And we get some extra icons here. So this one, the move allows you to click and drag it up or down. So if you want to move it to a different spot, you move it, that whatever that bar is, is where it's going to let it go. You can click on the settings, the gear icon, and this goes for any section to do things like change the font, change the color of the font, the backgrounds, all of that. And when you're done making those changes, you can just click back, it'll take you here. If you have something that you like and you want to make a copy of it, you can click that clone button right there. But we want to delete this one. So I'm going to click that trash can. It's going to ask you to are you sure? I'm gonna hit okay. And then that's gone. So we also want to make sure that this is customized for your gym. So you can change this tagline to whatever you like. But for instance, this has a location, you definitely don't want to leave location there. So you probably want to put in your city or your area. I'm going to click in here and I'm going to change the name. And say we're giving away, we have 30 spots. It's going to call this a 42 day challenge. And then once I'm done, I can click out of it. Now say I don't like that text, I'm going to hit the settings. Let's type it out section. Let's go and choose a different font. It's going to choose Oswald that doesn't look good. It's good and try. That one's fine. If you want to change the text color, you can the bold colors, what's going to be when the text is bold. And once I'm done, just click back here. This is a countdown timer that's going to add a sense of urgency. So if you click on that countdown timer, you can change how long it's going to give you. So if you want 30 minutes, 15 minutes, whatever works the best. This is looking pretty good. We do need to update the image here to wherever your gym happens to be. So make sure you just save that image to your computer. And then you click on the image. Click on that button right there next to image and you can upload it. Or if it's already here, you can just click on it and click add image and it puts it on. If you accidentally make a change you don't like, you do have an undo up here. So I'm going to hit undo and it changed it back. So that's looking pretty good. But there's something else we need to change or at least take a look at on this page. And that's going to be the pop up because when they click this button here, the send me my voucher button, it's going to bring over a pop over on their screen that they'll need to fill out. That's where they put in their information. So up at this bar at the top, we have pop up right there. And I'm going to go and click on edit settings. That's going to bring up this pop up and it's going to bring up what you can change inside of it. So if you want to have a image there instead of a white background, or you want to change the width of it or change the color, you can do that there. You also want to click in here and customize the text as needed. So we're calling this a 42 day challenge. So we want to click in here and change it to challenge. We also include some disclaimers inside of this pop up. Make sure this meets the requirements for your specific business, your unique business, consult with your legal counsel, if you're ever not sure. How we have it set up is that there's a disclaimer saying that by clicking that button, they're going to receive auto text calls or emails about the deal at that number and email provided. And that consent is not a condition to purchase anything. That's a pretty standard disclaimer. But again, make sure it meets the requirements for yours. This section here is I agree to the terms of use and privacy policy. And this is normally required whenever anybody is opting into anything. So you want to make sure that has the right language. And also if you notice, there's no check mark in that box by default. That means that they do have to check that box before they can go through. If you click on this section, it'll let you edit the text. Or if you hit the gear icon for it, you can change some settings here. So if you notice, we have it set to require that means they have to check it. That's what you want to see. So we've changed our page and we've changed our pop over. I don't see anything else I want to change on this. But make sure this is accurate for your gym. You're using good copy. And don't be afraid to change things and test things. You never know what's going to stop performing in the future, or what may start performing well in the future. So now that I've made my changes, I'm going to hit the save at the top right. Now we recommend saving twice for good measure. And I'm going to hit this preview button. This is going to let me see what it looks like on my screen. So I'm going to make this window really skinny. That way, adjust it like it was on a phone. And so this kind of gives you a good idea of what anybody on a phone is going to see this like on their page.
